---
title: "Lab 3: CSS Design and JavaScript Validation"
date: "2026-02-06"
due: "2026-02-11"
---


This lab covers workbook experiments 5 and 6.

## Workbook Alignment

- Experiment 5: Add a Cascading Style Sheet for designing the web page
- Experiment 6: Design a dynamic web page with validation using JavaScript

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this lab, you will be able to:

- connect an external stylesheet to an HTML page
- use selectors, colors, spacing, and layout rules effectively
- validate form inputs using JavaScript
- show inline error messages for invalid input
- create a complete static and dynamic front-end page

## Software and Files Required

- completion of Lab 2
- VS Code or any text editor
- a modern browser
- a folder named `Lab03`
- files named `index.html`, `style.css`, and `validation.html`

## Experiment 5: CSS Styling

### Problem Statement

Design a web page using an external CSS file.

### Folder Structure

```text
Lab03/
  index.html
  style.css
  validation.html
```

### Procedure

1. Create the `Lab03` folder.
2. Add the files `index.html` and `style.css`.
3. Link the stylesheet using the `<link>` tag.
4. Apply colors, spacing, cards, and layout rules from the CSS file.
5. Open the page in a browser and confirm that the styles load correctly.

### `index.html`

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>BMC201 Lab 3 - Styled Page</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
</head>
<body>
  <header class="banner">
    <h1>Web Technology Course Portal</h1>
    <p>Styled using an external CSS file</p>
  </header>

  <main class="container">
    <section class="card">
      <h2>Topics Covered</h2>
      <ul>
        <li>HTML structure</li>
        <li>CSS presentation</li>
        <li>JavaScript interactivity</li>
      </ul>
    </section>

    <section class="card">
      <h2>Why CSS Matters</h2>
      <p>CSS controls color, spacing, alignment, responsive behavior, and overall user experience.</p>
    </section>
  </main>
</body>
</html>
```

### `style.css`

```css
* {
  box-sizing: border-box;
}

body {
  margin: 0;
  font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
  background: #f8fafc;
  color: #1f2937;
}

.banner {
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0f766e, #0ea5e9);
  color: white;
  padding: 32px 20px;
  text-align: center;
}

.container {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px, 1fr));
  gap: 20px;
  max-width: 960px;
  margin: 24px auto;
  padding: 0 16px;
}

.card {
  background: white;
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 20px;
  box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.08);
}

li {
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}
```

### Expected Result

The browser should display a styled course portal with a gradient banner, responsive card layout, and clean spacing loaded from an external CSS file.

## Experiment 6: JavaScript Form Validation

### Problem Statement

Design a dynamic web page with validation using JavaScript.

### Procedure

1. Create a new file named `validation.html` in the `Lab03` folder.
2. Add a form containing name, email, and password fields.
3. Add error containers below each input.
4. Use JavaScript to validate user input on form submission.
5. Test the page with both valid and invalid values.

### Solution Code

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>BMC201 Lab 3 - Form Validation</title>
  <style>
    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      max-width: 680px;
      margin: 30px auto;
      padding: 0 16px;
    }

    .field {
      margin-bottom: 16px;
    }

    label {
      display: block;
      font-weight: bold;
      margin-bottom: 6px;
    }

    input {
      width: 100%;
      padding: 10px;
      border: 1px solid #cbd5e1;
      border-radius: 6px;
    }

    .error {
      color: #dc2626;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      margin-top: 4px;
    }

    .success {
      color: #047857;
      font-weight: bold;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Student Registration Form</h1>

  <form id="registrationForm" novalidate>
    <div class="field">
      <label for="name">Full Name</label>
      <input id="name" type="text">
      <div class="error" id="nameError"></div>
    </div>

    <div class="field">
      <label for="email">Email</label>
      <input id="email" type="email">
      <div class="error" id="emailError"></div>
    </div>

    <div class="field">
      <label for="password">Password</label>
      <input id="password" type="password">
      <div class="error" id="passwordError"></div>
    </div>

    <button type="submit">Submit</button>
  </form>

  <p id="message"></p>

  <script>
    const form = document.getElementById("registrationForm");
    const message = document.getElementById("message");

    form.addEventListener("submit", function (event) {
      event.preventDefault();

      const name = document.getElementById("name").value.trim();
      const email = document.getElementById("email").value.trim();
      const password = document.getElementById("password").value.trim();

      let isValid = true;

      document.getElementById("nameError").textContent = "";
      document.getElementById("emailError").textContent = "";
      document.getElementById("passwordError").textContent = "";
      message.textContent = "";
      message.className = "";

      if (name.length < 3) {
        document.getElementById("nameError").textContent = "Name must be at least 3 characters long.";
        isValid = false;
      }

      if (!email.includes("@") || !email.includes(".")) {
        document.getElementById("emailError").textContent = "Enter a valid email address.";
        isValid = false;
      }

      if (password.length < 6) {
        document.getElementById("passwordError").textContent = "Password must be at least 6 characters long.";
        isValid = false;
      }

      if (isValid) {
        message.textContent = "Form submitted successfully.";
        message.className = "success";
      }
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

### Expected Result

The form should display inline error messages for invalid input and show a success message only when all fields satisfy the validation rules.

## Observation Questions

1. What is the difference between internal CSS and external CSS?
2. Why is an external stylesheet useful when multiple pages share the same design?
3. Why is `event.preventDefault()` used in form validation?
4. What is the purpose of `trim()` in JavaScript?

## Viva Questions

1. Why should validation be done both on the client and the server?
2. What is the purpose of the `<link rel="stylesheet">` tag?
3. What is the difference between a class selector and an element selector?
4. How does JavaScript improve static HTML pages?

## Submission Checklist

- `index.html`
- `style.css`
- `validation.html`
- screenshots of styled page and validated form
- short answer file for observation and viva questions

## Extension Task

Add mobile number validation, move the JavaScript into a separate `script.js` file, and add a reset button for the form.
